summary: Completed testing of Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) push notifications integration. Backend API tests (12/12 passed, 2 skipped due to no non-admin user). Frontend loads and logs in correctly. Firebase SDK initializes at startup. Notification permission is denied in headless browser (expected behavior).
critical_code_review_comments: Notifications collection has mixed schema - old notifications use 'message' field, new FCM notifications use 'body' field. Consider migration or consistent field naming.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Firebase notifications integration tested and working. Backend endpoints /api/notifications/register-token, /api/notifications/send, and /api/notifications/history all return expected responses. Firebase Admin SDK initializes successfully at backend startup (check logs). Frontend Firebase SDK correctly attempts to request notification permission on login. Headless browsers will show 'Notification permission denied' which is expected. Dashboard stats confirmed: 89 students, 3 teachers, 4 courses, 4 departments.

tests_performed.notes: Returns 200 with 'no devices' message for fake user IDs
Returns 200 with list of 12 notification records
Logs show 'Firebase Admin SDK initialized successfully' at startup

tests_performed.test: Frontend: Login flow after Firebase integration

tests_performed.notes: Console shows 'Notification permission denied' - expected in headless browsers
